title: Failure mode = what it looks like., Reliability mechanism = why it happens, Root cause = where it started.
gist: A concise semiconductor-packaging reliability framework distinguishing failure modes (visible symptoms) from reliability mechanisms (underlying physics) from root cause (found only through cross-domain correlation), arguing advanced packaging needs root-cause evidence, not just defect detection.

insights.claim: In semiconductor packaging, failure modes are visible symptoms (wire bond lift-off, solder voids, die cracking, delamination, warpage, corrosion, opens, leakage), while reliability mechanisms explain the underlying physics and root cause is found only through correlation.
insights.speaker: M
insights.so_what: A clean three-layer diagnostic model for evaluating whether a packaging supplier is doing real reliability engineering versus surface-level defect screening.
insights.quote: Failure modes show the symptom. Reliability mechanisms reveal the physics. Correlation finds the root cause.
insights.locator: Advanced packaging needs more than defect detection

insights.claim: Different reliability stress tests expose distinct physical mechanisms: high-temperature storage reveals diffusion, intermetallic growth and Kirkendall voiding; THB/HAST reveals moisture ingress, ionic contamination and electrochemical corrosion; thermal cycling reveals CTE mismatch, fatigue and solder-joint stress.
insights.speaker: M
insights.so_what: Maps qualification tests to the failure physics they surface, useful for judging the rigor of a device's reliability qual program.

insights.claim: Root cause requires correlation across design, materials, wafer process, package architecture, OSAT assembly, inspection, electrical test, qualification and field behavior, because a visible defect is rarely caused only by the domain it appears in.
insights.speaker: M
insights.so_what: Reinforces that packaging failures are systems problems spanning the supply chain; a supplier without cross-domain correlation capability is a hidden reliability risk.
insights.quote: A solder void may not be only a solder problem. A bond failure may not be only a wire-bond problem.

insights.claim: Optical drift in co-packaged optics (CPO) may not be only a photonics problem, illustrating that emerging advanced-packaging technologies inherit multi-domain root-cause complexity.
insights.speaker: M
insights.so_what: Flags CPO reliability as an area where cross-disciplinary root-cause analysis will be essential as the technology scales.
insights.quote: An optical drift issue in CPO may not be only a photonics problem.
insights.locator: A bond failure may not be only a wire-bond problem
